---
title: "Tidy update: IOPAC-network-map-script-cciea"
format: html
---

```{r}
## --- Setup & Directories ---
library(tidyverse)
library(ggmap)
library(cowplot)
library(here)
library(magick)
```

```{r}
# Configuration
thisyear <- 2025
most_recent_igraph <- "2026-03-10"
png_pattern <- paste0("2025_circular_compare_", most_recent_igraph)

indir_ports <- 'data/networks/participation_vessel_ports/plots/comparable/2025 only'
esr_dir <- "/Users/ana.guerra/Documents/CONFIDENTIAL/Outputs/CCIEA Networks/Figures/"
esr_output <- TRUE
```

## --- Efficient Image Loading ---
```{r}
# Get all matching files at once
all_files <- list.files(
  file.path(indir_ports),
  pattern = png_pattern,
  full.names = TRUE
)

# Define a mapping of short names to search patterns
port_patterns <- c(
  nwc = "North WA Coast",
  ps = "Puget Sound",
  wc = "(?<!North )WA Coast",
  ast = "Astoria",
  tl = "Tillamook",
  nwp = "Newport",
  cb = "Coos Bay",
  brk = "Brookings",
  cr = "Columbia River",
  cc = "Crescent City",
  eu = "Eureka",
  fb = "Fort Bragg",
  bb = "Bodega Bay",
  sf = "San Francisco",
  mt = "Monterey",
  sd = "San Diego",
  sb = "Santa Barbara",
  la = "Los Angeles",
  mb = "Morro Bay"
)
```


# Load all images into a named list using functional programming

```{r}
# This replaces the failing block
imgs <- purrr::map(port_patterns, function(pat) {
  match <- all_files[stringr::str_detect(all_files, stringr::regex(pat))]

  if (length(match) > 0) {
    magick::image_read(match[1])
  } else {
    message(paste("Warning: No file found for port pattern:", pat))
    NULL
  }
})
```

```{r}
# Check for missing images
missing_ports <- names(imgs)[purrr::map_lgl(imgs, is.null)]
if (length(missing_ports) > 0) {
  cat(
    "The following ports failed to load:",
    paste(missing_ports, collapse = ", ")
  )
}
```


```{r}
# Load coordinate reference
box <- read_csv(here('data', 'input', 'node_box_ref_esr2026.csv'))
```


## --- Consolidated Mapping Logic ---

```{r}

poi <- c('Puget Sound', 'Newport', 'Crescent City', 'Eureka')


# Just creates the base map for the specified states, nothing else

base_map_iopac <- function(state, xlim = c(-130, -117), ylim = c(32, 49)) {
  states <- map_data("state")
  # Convert input to lowercase to match 'states$region'
  west_coast <- subset(states, region %in% tolower(state))

  if (nrow(west_coast) == 0) {
    stop("No states found. Check state name spelling.")
  }

  base_map <- ggplot(data = west_coast) +
    geom_polygon(
      aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
      fill = "gray90",
      alpha = 0.5,
      color = "gray85"
    ) +
    # Ensure coord_map is available (requires mapproj package)
    coord_map(xlim = xlim, ylim = ylim) +
    ggthemes::theme_map()

  return(base_map)
}
```

```{r}
box_iopac <- function(basemap, region_code) {
  # 'box' must be loaded in the global environment
  region_boxes <- box %>% filter(map_version == region_code)

  basemap +
    geom_point(
      data = region_boxes,
      aes(PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, PRIMARY_LATITUDE, fill = STATE),
      colour = "black",
      pch = 21,
      size = 2
    ) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = region_boxes,
      aes(PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, PRIMARY_LATITUDE, label = IOPAC),
      size = 2.15,
      box.padding = 0.5 # Increased slightly to prevent overlap
    ) +
    geom_rect(
      data = region_boxes,
      aes(xmax = xmax, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, ymin = ymin),
      fill = NA,
      colour = "darkgray"
    ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = region_boxes,
      aes(
        x = PRIMARY_LONGITUDE,
        y = PRIMARY_LATITUDE,
        xend = xmax,
        yend = ymax
      ),
      color = 'darkgray'
    ) +
    # Ensure values match the number of unique states in your data
    scale_fill_manual(values = c('gray', 'black', 'white')) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}
```

```{r}
box_iopac2 <- function(basemap, region_code) {
  # 'box' must be loaded in the global environment
  region_boxes <- box %>% filter(map_version == region_code)

  basemap +
    geom_point(
      data = region_boxes,
      aes(PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, PRIMARY_LATITUDE, fill = STATE),
      colour = "black",
      pch = 21,
      size = 2
    ) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = region_boxes,
      aes(PRIMARY_LONGITUDE, PRIMARY_LATITUDE, label = IOPAC),
      size = 2.15,
      box.padding = 0.5 # Increased slightly to prevent overlap
    ) +
    # Ensure values match the number of unique states in your data
    scale_fill_manual(values = c('gray', 'black', 'white')) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}
```

## --- Generate and Save Coastwide Figure ---


```{r}
draw_image_border <- function(
  x,
  y,
  width,
  height,
  color = "darkgray",
  size = 1
) {
  # Create a completely empty plot with only a border
  border_plot <- ggplot() +
    theme_void() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(colour = color, fill = NA, size = size))

  # Draw that "border-only" plot at the same location as your image
  cowplot::draw_plot(border_plot, x = x, y = y, width = width, height = height)
}

```

```{r}
base_p <- box_iopac2(
  base_map_iopac(c("washington", "oregon", "california")),
  'CW'
)

CW_map <- cowplot::ggdraw() +

  # 2. Draw the image at the EXACT same coordinates
  # cowplot::draw_image(imgs$ps, x = .1, y = .66, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$ps) +
  cowplot::draw_label(
    "Puget Sound Inset",
    x = 0.5,
    y = 0.88,
    fontface = 'bold',
    size = 10
  )

CW_map
```

```{r}
base_p <- box_iopac(
  base_map_iopac(c("washington", "oregon", "california")),
  'CW'
)

# 2. Use ggdraw to layer images and coordinate-matching rectangles
CW_map <- cowplot::ggdraw(base_p) +
  # --- Puget Sound ---
  cowplot::draw_image(
    imgs$ps,
    x = get_coord("CW", "Puget Sound", "draw_x"),
    y = get_coord("CW", "Puget Sound", "draw_y"),
    width = 0.31,
    height = 0.31
  )

CW_map

```


```{r}

base_p <- box_iopac(
  base_map_iopac(c("washington", "oregon", "california")),
  'CW'
)

# 2. Use ggdraw to layer images and coordinate-matching rectangles
cowplot::ggdraw(base_p) +
  # --- Puget Sound ---
  cowplot::draw_image(
    imgs$ps,
    x = get_coord("CW", "Puget Sound", "draw_x") + 0.22,
    y = get_coord("CW", "Puget Sound", "draw_y") - 0.03,
    width = 0.25,
    height = 0.25
  )


```

  ```{r}

# --- Newport ---
cowplot::draw_rect(
  x = .08,
  y = .43,
  width = 0.31,
  height = 0.31,
  color = "darkgray",
  fill = NA
) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$nwp, x = .08, y = .43, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +

  # --- Crescent City ---
  cowplot::draw_rect(
    x = .08,
    y = .18,
    width = 0.31,
    height = 0.31,
    color = "darkgray",
    fill = NA
  ) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$cc, x = .08, y = .18, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +

  # --- Eureka ---
  cowplot::draw_rect(
    x = .27,
    y = .29,
    width = 0.31,
    height = 0.31,
    color = "darkgray",
    fill = NA
  ) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$eu, x = .27, y = .29, width = 0.31, height = 0.31)

# Display the map
CW_map

`
`
`
`
``
{
  r
}

cw_region_boxes <- box %>% filter(map_version == 'CW')


CW_map <- box_iopac2(
  base_map_iopac(c("washington", "oregon", "california")),
  'CW'
) +
  geom_rect(
    data = cw_region_boxes,
    aes(xmax = xmax, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, ymin = ymin),
    fill = NA,
    colour = "darkgray"
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = cw_region_boxes,
    aes(
      x = PRIMARY_LONGITUDE,
      y = PRIMARY_LATITUDE,
      xend = xmax,
      yend = ymax
    ),
    color = 'darkgray'
  ) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$ps, x = .06, y = .66, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$nwp, x = .08, y = .43, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$cc, x = .08, y = .18, width = 0.31, height = 0.31) +
  cowplot::draw_image(imgs$eu, x = .27, y = .29, width = 0.31, height = 0.31)

```

```{r}
CW_map
`
`
`
`
``
{
  r
}
# Improved save_map helper
save_map <- function(p, name, w = 6, h = 10) {
  # Use file.path for cleaner cross-platform paths
  local_path <- here::here(indir_ports, paste0(name, '_', thisyear, '.png'))
  ggsave(local_path, p, width = w, height = h, dpi = 600)

  if (exists("esr_output") && esr_output == TRUE) {
    ggsave(
      file.path(esr_dir, paste0(name, '-', thisyear, '.png')),
      p,
      width = w,
      height = h,
      dpi = 600
    )
  }
}

save_map(CW_map, "iopac-node_full-coast_rect_font")
```
